User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Noether application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

college.description: College or university the user attends.

degree.description: Degree program the user is pursuing.

summary.description: A brief biography or summary about the user.

projects.description: List of projects the user is working on or has completed (markdown style).

profileImage.description: URL or path to the user's profile image.

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Represents a document uploaded by the user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

fileName.description: Original name of the uploaded file.

fileType.description: MIME type of the uploaded file (e.g., application/pdf, application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.presentationml.presentation).

uploadDate.description: Date and time when the file was uploaded.

summary.description: AI-generated summary of the document content.

importantTopics.description: List of important topics extracted from the document by AI.

flashcardIds.description: References to Flashcards generated from this document. (Relationship: Document 1:N Flashcard)

flowchart.description: AI-generated flowchart representation of the document content (e.g., SVG or JSON format).

Flashcard.title: Flashcard
Flashcard.description: Represents a flashcard generated from a document.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Flashcard entity.

question.description: Question or prompt on the flashcard.

answer.description: Answer to the question or prompt on the flashcard.

PastYearQuestion.title: PastYearQuestion
PastYearQuestion.description: Represents a past year question paper.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PastYearQuestion entity.

fileName.description: Name of the uploaded PYQ file.

uploadDate.description: Date and time the PYQ file was uploaded.

questions.description: An array of questions contained in the PYQ.

answers.description: An array of answers generated for the PYQ.

Timetable.title: Timetable
Timetable.description: Represents a study timetable generated by the user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Timetable entity.

subjectHours.description: JSON stringified format which tracks how many hours per week are dedicated to the subject. (e.g., {math:5,physics:3})

generatedDate.description: Date and time the timetable was generated.

timetableData.description: JSON stringified representation of the timetable.

TodoTask.title: TodoTask
TodoTask.description: Represents a task in the to-do list.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TodoTask entity.

completed.description: Indicates whether the task has been completed.

VoiceNote.title: VoiceNote
VoiceNote.description: Represents a voice note recorded by the user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the VoiceNote entity.

text.description: Text transcribed from the voice note.

createdDate.description: Date and time the voice note was created.

GameScore.title: GameScore
GameScore.description: Represents the score for a game played by the user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GameScore entity.

gameName.description: Name of the game (e.g., Sudoku, Memory).

playedDate.description: Date and time the game was played.

definition.description: Stores user profile data.  Authorization is based on matching {userId} with request.auth.uid.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores documents uploaded by the user. Authorization is based on matching {userId} with request.auth.uid.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the document.

definition.description: Stores flashcards generated from a document. Authorization is based on matching {userId} with request.auth.uid.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the document.

params.name: flashcardId
params.description: The unique identifier of the flashcard.

definition.description: Stores past year question papers uploaded by the user. Authorization is based on matching {userId} with request.auth.uid.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: pastYearQuestionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the past year question.

definition.description: Stores timetables generated by the user. Authorization is based on matching {userId} with request.auth.uid.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: timetableId
params.description: The unique identifier of the timetable.

definition.description: Stores to-do tasks created by the user. Authorization is based on matching {userId} with request.auth.uid.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: todoTaskId
params.description: The unique identifier of the to-do task.

definition.description: Stores voice notes recorded by the user. Authorization is based on matching {userId} with request.auth.uid.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: voiceNoteId
params.description: The unique identifier of the voice note.

definition.description: Stores game scores achieved by the user. Authorization is based on matching {userId} with request.auth.uid.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: gameScoreId
params.description: The unique identifier of the game score.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure is designed to follow best practices for security, scalability, and debuggability, focusing on authorization independence and clear data ownership. The structure utilizes path-based ownership for user-specific data and avoids hierarchical authorization dependencies by denormalizing authorization context where needed. This approach ensures that security rules are straightforward and efficient, and that atomic operations can be performed without requiring complex `get()` calls. Homogeneous security postures are ensured by structurally segregating data based on access needs. The application uses path-based ownership for entities like Documents, Flashcards, Timetables, TodoTasks, VoiceNotes, and GameScores, all nested under the `/users/{userId}` collection, which provides a simple and secure way to manage user-specific data. This approach means that `list` operations are inherently secure as they are scoped to the user's path. Additionally, there is no need for custom claims, and all authorization relies solely on `request.auth.uid`.
